name: Fourleaf
description_short: FourLeaf Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, mortgages, auto loans, and investment services to personal and business members.
description_long:
FourLeaf Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union serving both personal and business members. As a not-for-profit, member-owned institution insured by NCUA, FourLeaf operates under a cooperative model where members are technically part-owners rather than customers of a traditional bank. The credit union has established itself as a full-service financial institution with a significant presence indicated by their substantial routing number infrastructure and national toll-free support line.

FourLeaf offers a comprehensive suite of financial products spanning personal and business banking. On the personal side, they provide checking and savings accounts (including student accounts), certificates of deposit, money market accounts, IRAs, and specialized savings products. Their lending portfolio includes auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, credit cards, and student loan refinancing. The business division mirrors these offerings with business checking, savings, money market, certificates, and specialized lending including business credit cards, auto loans, lines of credit, term loans, and commercial real estate lending. They've integrated third-party services like Zelle for payments and partnered with FourLeaf Financial Group for wealth management and insurance products.

FourLeaf distinguishes itself through several member-centric offerings absent from traditional banks. They provide free credit counseling, financial learning centers covering autos, credit, mortgages, and HELOCs, and free seminars and webinars. Their "Retirement Simplified" program powered by Silvur, along with portfolio review and college planning services, suggests a commitment to member financial education and long-term planning. The business banking offerings include a "Bank at Work" program and succession planning services, indicating outreach to small business communities.

As a credit union, FourLeaf's primary limitation is membership eligibility—unlike banks, credit unions restrict services to qualifying members based on geographic area, employment, or other criteria not detailed on their public website. Additionally, while their digital banking capabilities exist, their online presence does not highlight competitive rate advantages or technology innovations that might differentiate them from larger financial institutions. The breadth of offerings suggests a regional or mid-sized credit union, but member feedback on service quality, rate competitiveness, and loan approval ease is not evident from their marketing materials alone.
services: Personal Checking Accounts (Free Checking, Student Checking)
Personal Savings Accounts (Savings, Money Market, Certificates, Student Savings, IRAs)
Business Lending (Lines of Credit, Term Loans, Commercial Real Estate, Business Auto Loans)
Investment Services and Wealth Management (Guided Wealth Portfolios, Portfolio Review, College Planning, Retirement Planning)
Insurance Services (Auto, Homeowners, Life Insurance, Debt Protection)
pros: Comprehensive product suite covering savings, checking, mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and business lending all in-house
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means earnings can theoretically benefit members through better rates and lower fees
Free credit counseling and financial education resources including dedicated learning centers for autos, mortgages, credit, and HELOCs
Full business banking division with CRE lending, term loans, business credit cards, and succession planning services
Integrated investment and insurance services through FourLeaf Financial Group, including wealth management and retirement planning
Digital banking capabilities including online banking, mobile banking, and virtual appointment services
NCUA insurance provides member account protection (similar to FDIC for banks)
cons: Membership eligibility requirements restrict access—specific membership criteria are not transparently detailed on their website
No mention of rate competitiveness or whether their rates are market-leading compared to national banks or online credit unions
Digital banking features mentioned but not demonstrated in detail, unlike competitors who showcase user interfaces and functionality
Limited information about approval timelines, loan terms, or specific product fees on their public-facing website
Geographic or employment-based membership restrictions likely limit accessibility for many consumers without clear eligibility pathways listed
best_for: Small business owners seeking comprehensive banking, lending, and wealth management services from a single institution
Members eligible by geography or employment who prioritize credit counseling, financial education, and member-owned structure
Homebuyers and refinancers looking for mortgages, HELOCs, and mortgage pre-qualification services with educational support
Personal loan and auto loan borrowers who value free financial literacy resources and member-owned governance
diagnosis: FourLeaf is best for members who are eligible and seeking a comprehensive financial institution that combines lending, checking, savings, mortgages, and investment services with a commitment to financial education and member-owned governance. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—access depends on meeting specific criteria (geography, employment, etc.) that are not fully transparent on their public website, making it essential for prospective members to verify eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Fourleaf — Credit Union in Bethpage, NY | CreditDoc
meta_description: Fourleaf — NCUA-insured credit union in Bethpage, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
